denial_letter_text:
Dear Ms. Member,

UnitedHealthcare has reviewed the prior authorization request submitted by your orthopedic surgeon for a hip arthroscopy with labral repair and is unable to approve coverage at this time.

The request was received at 10:02 AM and this determination was issued at 10:05 AM the same day. Prior authorization for the procedure is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed within the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission.

Standard criteria apply to all requests of this type, and this determination is based on the request as adjudicated by the plan and not on an individual review of the submitted records.

To request reconsideration, your provider may submit a revised request consistent with the plan's procedures described in your plan documents. A revised request should include documentation that the plan's procedural requirements for a peer-to-peer review have been satisfied.

If you disagree with this determination, you or your provider may file an internal appeal by contacting the number on your member identification card within the timeframe stated in your plan documents. You or your provider may submit additional clinical records and a written statement with that request.

This determination addresses coverage under your plan only and is not medical advice.

Sincerely,
UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 34-year-old woman with a left hip labral tear and femoroacetabular impingement. She has reproducible anterior hip and groin pain with positive anterior impingement (FADIR) testing, and MRI arthrography shows an anterosuperior labral tear with an adjacent paralabral cyst. She completed more than three months of supervised physical therapy and activity modification and a diagnostic intra-articular anesthetic injection that transiently relieved her pain, confirming the intra-articular source. Her orthopedic surgeon submitted a detailed letter of medical necessity documenting the failed conservative care, the positive impingement testing, the MRI findings, and the diagnostic injection response, together with the request for hip arthroscopy with labral repair. That letter of medical necessity was part of the submitted file. The denial was issued three minutes after the request was received and does not address the MRI findings, the failed physical therapy, or any content of the physician's letter. She is otherwise healthy and an appropriate surgical candidate.

exploitable_weaknesses: Three-minute turnaround (10:02 received, 10:05 issued) with explicit admission it is not based on an individual review of the submitted records shows no genuine review occurred
Denial blames a missing peer-to-peer that the plan never afforded time for, and ignores the MRI findings, failed PT, and diagnostic injection in the submitted letter of medical necessity
strong_defenses: The plan can assert a facial procedural requirement (peer-to-peer not completed) as a nominal basis for reconsideration
